display.purpose: Window-level titles only; never larger than 22px.

title.purpose: Snippet titles in lists and primary labels.

body.purpose: Paragraph copy and snippet preview text.

label.purpose: Section labels, field notes, small button text.

kbd.purpose: Inline keyboard-shortcut hints in monospace, only inside .kbd chips.

motion.name: transition-state
motion.purpose: State changes on background-color, border-color, color, opacity. Default for buttons, list items, inputs.

motion.name: fade-in
motion.purpose: Component mount fade. Defined in @/lib/motion fadeIn variant.

motion.name: slide-up
motion.purpose: Panel and screen-section entrances. Defined in @/lib/motion slideUp variant.

motion.name: list-stagger
motion.purpose: List/grid stagger. Defined in @/lib/motion staggerContainer + listItem variants.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary call-to-action. Carries the warm accent. Reserved for the single most-important action in any region.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.16 0.01 260); background: oklch(0.82 0.03 78); border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), opacity 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.92); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.56); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary action. Hairline border on the cool surface; never carries the warm accent.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.97 0.01 85); background: transparent; border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14); border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), border-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: oklch(0.40 0.018 250 / 0.34); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.56); }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary action. Sits on dense rows where a button must exist but must not draw the eye.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 44px; padding: 8px 16px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.78 0.012 85); background: transparent; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: oklch(0.40 0.018 250 / 0.34); color: oklch(0.97 0.01 85); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.56); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default text field. Same height and radius as buttons so adjacent fields and actions align.
components.css: .ds-input-text { display: block; width: 100%; height: 44px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.97 0.01 85); background: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.10); border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.14); border-radius: 10px; outline: none; transition: border-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), box-shadow 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-input-text::placeholder { color: oklch(0.78 0.012 85); } .ds-input-text:focus-visible { border-color: oklch(0.82 0.03 78); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.56); } .ds-input-text:disabled { opacity: 0.5; }

components.name: List Item
components.description: Snippet row. Two text lines (title + truncated value), trailing meta chip. Active row carries the warm-accent border.
components.html:
Release branch cut
git checkout -b release/$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
components.css: .ds-list-item { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid transparent; border-radius: 8px; transition: background-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), border-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1), color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-list-item:hover { background: oklch(0.36 0.018 252 / 0.22); border-color: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.11); } .ds-list-item--active { background: oklch(0.44 0.025 252 / 0.22); border-color: oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.32); } .ds-list-item__title { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(0.97 0.01 85); line-height: 1.4; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-list-item__value { font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 400; color: oklch(0.78 0.012 85); line-height: 1.4; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }

components.name: Status Pill (Success)
components.description: Confirmation pill. Always paired with a label; never appears as a hue alone.
components.html: Accessibility ready
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 4px 10px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.15); background: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.06); color: oklch(0.95 0.01 85); border-radius: 14px; } .ds-pill--success { border-color: oklch(0.83 0.12 162 / 0.28); background: oklch(0.57 0.09 162 / 0.22); color: oklch(0.95 0.04 162); } .ds-pill--warning { border-color: oklch(0.86 0.13 92 / 0.28); background: oklch(0.62 0.11 92 / 0.18); color: oklch(0.97 0.04 95); } .ds-pill--error { border-color: oklch(0.8 0.13 28 / 0.28); background: oklch(0.62 0.12 28 / 0.18); color: oklch(0.95 0.03 30); }

components.name: Keyboard Shortcut Chip
components.description: Inline keyboard-shortcut hint. Monospace; lives next to the action it triggers.
components.css: .ds-kbd { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 1px 6px; font-family: 'SF Mono', ui-monospace, 'Cascadia Code', 'Fira Code', monospace; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: oklch(0.83 0.012 85); background: oklch(0.27 0.014 255 / 0.38); border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.12); border-radius: 6px; }

components.name: Inline Notice (Warning)
components.description: Inline alert for missing-permission, missing-hotkey, or other gentle blockers. Title + detail + recovery in one block.
components.html:
Accessibility permission required
QuickCommand needs Accessibility access to paste snippets into other apps.
components.css: .ds-notice { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; border-radius: 16px; border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.15); background: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.06); color: oklch(0.95 0.01 85); font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-notice--warning { border-color: oklch(0.86 0.13 92 / 0.28); background: oklch(0.62 0.11 92 / 0.18); color: oklch(0.97 0.04 95); } .ds-notice--error { border-color: oklch(0.8 0.13 28 / 0.28); background: oklch(0.62 0.12 28 / 0.18); color: oklch(0.95 0.03 30); } .ds-notice__title { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-notice__detail { font-size: 13px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.5; opacity: 0.92; } .ds-notice__action { align-self: flex-start; height: 32px; padding: 4px 12px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: inherit; background: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.10); border: 1px solid oklch(1 0 0 / 0.16); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1); } .ds-notice__action:hover { background: oklch(1 0 0 / 0.16); } .ds-notice__action:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.82 0.03 78 / 0.56); }

narrative.overview:
QuickCommand is a small, precise instrument made for someone who values craft. The visual system reads like a tool, not a product. Surfaces are quiet, dark, and cool, sized to the keyboard and the act of typing. The single warm accent, Late-Light Sand, lands on the one element the eye should travel to: the active row, the focused input, the primary button. Everything else gets out of the way.

The system is dark by default because QuickCommand lives on top of other apps, summoned and dismissed across the day. A pale UI would shout against terminals, editors, and runbook panes; a deep cool surface lets it sit beneath those workflows without drawing attention until the user wants it. Type is restrained, dense, and informational. Motion is brief, easing in and out without choreography. Color carries meaning, never decoration.

This system explicitly rejects the visual languages PRODUCT.md names as anti-references: SaaS-dashboard card grids and gradient accents, Sequoia-era over-frosted glass and decorative `backdrop-filter`, cloud-app maximalism, and toy/playful illustration. The current `styles.css` carries some legacy `backdrop-filter` rules; this document treats those as drift to remove, not pattern to extend.
keyCharacteristics: Dark cool blue-grey base with a single warm accent that earns every appearance.
Tonal layering for depth, not shadows or blur.
Apple system type at functional sizes, no display-scale headlines.
Generous keyboard targets (44px minimum) without visual bulk.
Status conveyed by labelled pills, never by hue alone.

rules.name: The One Warm Note Rule
rules.body: Late-Light Sand appears on at most one element per visible region, and never as a fill on more than ~10% of any screen at rest. If two elements both want it, only the higher-intent one (focus > selection > brand) gets it.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black, No-Pure-White Rule
rules.body: Backgrounds tilt cool (oklch(... 0.01–0.014 252–260)), text tilts warm (oklch(... 0.01 85)). Pure #000 and #fff are forbidden in all new styles.

rules.name: The Color-Plus-Label Rule
rules.body: Status colors never carry meaning alone. A success pill has both green and the word 'Accessibility ready'. A warning pill has both amber and the missing-state copy. Color blindness, dark adaptation, and ambient contrast all degrade hue; the label is what survives.

rules.name: The Functional-Scale Rule
rules.body: Body type stays at 15px. Display caps at 22px. The full ladder spans 10px (kbd) to 22px (display): one order of magnitude, no marketing leap. If the design wants more visual weight, it earns it with weight contrast (400 → 600), not with a 36px headline.

rules.name: The Two-Step Weight Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy uses two weights: 400 for body, 600 for emphasis. 500 exists for titles where 600 would shout. Never reach for 700 or italics.

rules.name: The Mono-Is-A-Cue Rule
rules.body: Monospace appears only in .kbd shortcut chips. Code blocks, snippet values, and file paths stay in the system sans, because snippets contain prose and code interleaved.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces have no box-shadow. Depth is read from the four-tier tonal ladder. If a designer feels the urge to add shadow for 'lift', they reach for the next tonal tier instead.

rules.name: The No-Glass Rule
rules.body: backdrop-filter and -webkit-backdrop-filter are forbidden on all new components. The macOS-native vibrancy of the menu-bar popover chrome is provided by the OS, not by the renderer; never reproduce it in CSS.

rules.name: The Inset-Below-Background Rule
rules.body: Recessed containers sit darker than background, not lighter. A snippet preview well, a scroll inset, a pressed input ground all use Surface Inset, which is below background lightness. This makes 'stacked above' and 'carved into' visually distinct.

dos: Do use Late-Light Sand on focus rings, primary buttons, and active list items, and only those surfaces by default.
Do reach for the next tonal tier when you want lift; tonal layering is the only depth mechanism.
Do keep 44px (h-11) as the minimum height of any interactive surface that takes pointer or keyboard focus.
Do pair every status hue with a label. 'Accessibility ready', not a green dot. 'No hotkey configured', not a yellow dot.
Do use OKLCH for every color value, including alpha overlays.
Do keep type at functional sizes (10px to 22px ladder) and lean on weight contrast (400 vs 600) for hierarchy.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion. The 140ms easing transitions are already governed by the global query in styles.css; new transitions must not opt out.
Do keep monospace inside .kbd chips and nowhere else.
Do keep snippet previews to two lines (title and value) and let the card body breathe.
donts: Don't use box-shadow on app surfaces. Tonal layering replaces it. The OS provides window-level shadows; nothing in the renderer should add more.

Don't use backdrop-filter or -webkit-backdrop-filter on new components. The 'Heavy glass and blur effects' anti-reference in PRODUCT.md is enforced here. Existing legacy rules in styles.css are drift to remove, not pattern to extend.
Don't introduce a marketing-scale headline. 22px is the ceiling; no display fonts, no hero numbers.
Don't build the SaaS-dashboard card grid: identical-shape tiles with icon + heading + body, repeated across the canvas.
Don't use side-stripe borders (border-left greater than 1px as colored accent). If a row needs emphasis, lift the background tier or apply the warm-accent border on all four sides.
Don't use gradient text or background-clip: text. Emphasis comes from weight and size.
Don't use pure #000 or #fff. Always tint cool (background) or warm (text).
Don't add cloud-app chrome: presence avatars, share buttons, sync indicators, comment threads. This is a local-first app and must not pretend otherwise.
Don't ship toy or doodled empty states. Empty states are one terse sentence and the next single action.
Don't reach for a modal as the first answer to 'where does this UI go?'. Inline editing, the palette as a focused-flow surface, and the tray as a quick-glance surface already cover the work.
Don't introduce a new accent hue. The system has one warm note. If you need a second emphasis, raise type weight or size.
Don't animate CSS layout properties (width, height, top, left, padding, margin). Use opacity and transform only.
